briefdescription.para: These constants specify the options that may be set or retrieved with the driver, each is a unique bit mask such that multiple options may be specified.

detaileddescription.para: These constants indicate the available options in active state.Starts break transmission

description.para: The UART can operate in one of four modes: Normal, Local Loopback, Remote Loopback, or automatic echo.
These constants specify the data format that the driver supports.
The data format includes the number of data bits, the number of stop bits and parity.
These constants specify the handler events that an application can handle using its specific handler function.
Note that these constants are not bit mask, so only one event can be passed to an application at a time.

briefdescription.para: A receive parity, frame,break error detected.

description.para: Register offsets for the UART.
The Receive status register/error clear register (UARTRSR) gives the status of break, frame, parity and overrun.
Receive status register/error clear register Bit Definition
The Flag register (UARTFR)
Flag Register Bit Definition
The Line Control register (UARTLCR) controls the functions of the device.
Line Control Register Bit Definition
The Control register (UARTCR) controls the major functions of the device.
Control Register Bit Definition
The UARTIFLS Register is the interrupt FIFO level select register.
You can use this register to define the FIFO level that triggers the assertion of UARTTXINTR and UARTRXINTR.
Interrupt FIFO Level Select Register Register Bit Definition

briefdescription.para: Receive interrupt FIFO level select mask.
Transmit interrupt FIFO level select shift.
Receive interrupt FIFO level select mask.
Transmit interrupt FIFO level select shift.

detaileddescription.para: 1/8 full
1/4 full
1/2 full
3/4 full
7/8 full
1/8 full
1/4 full
1/2 full
3/4 full
7/8 full

description.para: The UARTIMSC Register is the interrupt mask set/clear register.

It is a read/write register. On a read this register returns the current value of the mask on the relevant interrupt. On a write of 1 to the particular bit, it sets the corresponding mask of that interrupt. A write of 0 clears the corresponding mask.
Interrupt Mask Set/Clear Register Bit Definition
The raw interrupt status register (UARTRIS) is a read-only register.
This register returns the current raw status value, prior to masking, of the corresponding interrupt. A write has no effect.
Raw Interrupt Status Register Bit Definition
The UARTMIS Register is the masked interrupt status register.
It is a read-only register. This register returns the current masked status value of the corresponding interrupt. A write has no effect.
Masked Interrupt Status Register Bit Definition
The UARTICR Register is the interrupt clear register and is write-only.
On a write of 1, the corresponding interrupt is cleared. A write of 0 has no effect.
Interrupt Clear Register Bit Definition
The UARTDMACR Register is the DMA control register.
It is a read/write register. All the bits are cleared to 0 on reset.
DMA Control Register Bit Definition
The UARTIBRD Register is the integer part of the baud rate divisor value
The UARTFBRD Register is the fractional part of the baud rate divisor value

Baud rate divisor BAUDDIV = (FUARTCLK/(16xBaud rate)) where FUARTCLK is the UART reference clock frequency The BAUDDIV is comprised of the integer value (BAUD DIVINT) and the fractional value (BAUD DIVFRAC)
Use the receiver timeout register (RTR) to detect an idle condition on the receiver data line.
Use the Receiver FIFO Trigger Level Register (RTRIG) to set the value at which the RX FIFO triggers an interrupt event.
Use the Transmit FIFO Trigger Level Register (TTRIG) to set the value at which the TX FIFO triggers an interrupt event.
This register (MODEMCR) controls the interface with the modem or data set, or a peripheral device emulating a modem.
This register (MODEMSR) indicates the current state of the control lines from a modem, or another peripheral device, to the CPU.
In addition, four bits of the modem status register provide change information. These bits are set to a logic 1 whenever a control input from the modem changes state.
Note: Whenever the DCTS, DDSR, TERI, or DDCD bit is set to logic 1, a modem status interrupt is generated and this is reflected in the modem status register.
Operation of the flow delay register (FLOWDEL) is very similar to the receive FIFO trigger register.

An internal trigger signal activates when the FIFO is filled to the level set by this register. This trigger will not cause an interrupt, although it can be read through the channel status register. In hardware flow control mode, RTS is deactivated when the trigger becomes active. RTS only resets when the FIFO level is four less than the level of the flow delay trigger and the flow delay trigger is not activated. A value less than 4 disables the flow delay.
The Receiver FIFO Status register is used to have a continuous monitoring of the raw unmasked byte status information.
The register contains frame, parity and break status information for the top four bytes in the RX FIFO.
Receiver FIFO Byte Status Register Bit Definition

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* XUartPsv_Handler)(void *CallBackRef, u32 Event, u32 EventData)
memberdef.name: XUartPsv_Handler

briefdescription.para: This data type defines a handler that an application defines to communicate with interrupt system to retrieve state information about an application.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the handler, and is passed back to the upper layer when the handler is called. It is used to find the device driver instance.
contains one of the event constants indicating events that have occurred.
contains the number of bytes sent or received at the time of the call for send and receive events and contains the modem status for modem events.

memberdef.name: XUartPsv_OptionsTable
memberdef.initializer: = { {XUARTPSV_OPTION_SET_BREAK, XUARTPSV_UARTCR_OFFSET, XUARTPSV_UARTLCR_BRK}, {XUARTPSV_OPTION_STOP_BREAK, XUARTPSV_UARTCR_OFFSET, XUARTPSV_UARTLCR_BRK}, {XUARTPSV_OPTION_RESET_TMOUT, XUARTPSV_UARTCR_OFFSET, XUARTPSV_UARTCR_RTSEN}, {XUARTPSV_OPTION_RESET_TX, XUARTPSV_UARTCR_OFFSET, XUARTPSV_UARTFR_TXFE}, {XUARTPSV_OPTION_RESET_RX, XUARTPSV_UARTCR_OFFSET, XUARTPSV_UARTFR_RXFE}, {XUARTPSV_OPTION_ASSERT_RTS, XUARTPSV_UARTCR_OFFSET, XUARTPSV_MODEMCR_RTS}, {XUARTPSV_OPTION_ASSERT_DTR, XUARTPSV_UARTCR_OFFSET, XUARTPSV_MODEMCR_DTR}, {XUARTPSV_OPTION_SET_FCM, XUARTPSV_UARTCR_OFFSET, XUARTPSV_MODEMCR_FCM} }

briefdescription.para: This function is a stub handler that is the default handler such that if the application has not set the handler when interrupts are enabled, this function will be called.

parameterdescription.para: is unused by this function.
is unused by this function.
is unused by this function.

briefdescription.para: Initializes a specific XUartPsv instance such that it is ready to be used.

detaileddescription.para: The data format of the device is setup for 8 data bits, 1 stop bit, and no parity by default. The baud rate is set to a default value specified by Config->DefaultBaudRate if set, otherwise it is set to 19.2K baud. The receive FIFO threshold is set for 8 bytes. The default operating mode of the driver is polled mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance.
is a reference to a structure containing information about a specific XUartPsv driver.

is the device base address in the virtual memory address space. The caller is responsible for keeping the address mapping from EffectiveAddr to the device physical base address unchanged once this function is invoked. Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping changes after this function is called. If address translation is not used, pass in the physical address instead.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if initialization was successful
XST_UART_BAUD_ERROR if the baud rate is not possible because the inputclock frequency is not divisible with an acceptable amount of error

simplesect.para: The default configuration for the UART after initialization is:

listitem.para: 19,200 bps or XPAR_DFT_BAUDRATE if defined
8 data bits
1 stop bit
no parity
FIFO's are enabled with a threshold of 16 ytes
All interrupts are disabled.

briefdescription.para: This functions sends the specified buffer using the device in either polled or interrupt driven mode.

detaileddescription.para: This function is non-blocking, if the device is busy sending data, it will return and indicate zero bytes were sent. Otherwise, it fills the TX FIFO as much as it can, and return the number of bytes sent.
In a polled mode, this function will only send as much data as TX FIFO can buffer. The application may need to call it repeatedly to send the entire buffer.

In interrupt mode, this function will start sending the specified buffer, then the interrupt handler will continue sending data until the entire buffer has been sent. A callback function, as specified by the application, will be called to indicate the completion of sending.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance.
is pointer to a buffer of data to be sent.
contains the number of bytes to be sent. A value of zero will stop a previous send operation that is in progress in interrupt mode. Any data that was already put into the transmit FIFO will be sent.

simplesect.para: The number of bytes actually sent.
The number of bytes is not asserted so that this function may be called with a value of zero to stop an operation that is already in progress.

listitem.para: This function sends a buffer that has been previously specified by setting
up the instance variables of the instance.
This function implemented for RTOS environment and it is an internal
function for the XUartPsv driver such that it's being called from
XUartPsv_Send_NoWait function that sets up the buffer.
This function sends the specified buffer in polled mode. This function
only sends as much data as the TX FIFO can buffer. The application may
need to call it repeatedly to send the entire buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance

simplesect.para: The number of bytes actually sent

listitem.para: This functions sends the specified buffer using the device in polled mode.
This function is non-blocking, if the device is busy sending data, it will
return and indicate zero bytes were sent. Otherwise, it fills the TX FIFO
as much as it can, and return the number of bytes sent. The application may
need to call it repeatedly to send the entire buffer.
This function implemented for RTOS environment, it will check status and
return without waiting. The caller of XUartPsv_Send_NoWait can wait
based on return value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance.
is pointer to a buffer of data to be sent.
contains the number of bytes to be sent. A value of

listitem.para: zero will stop a previous send operation that is in progress
in interrupt mode. Any data that was already put into the
transmit FIFO will be sent.

simplesect.para: - The number of bytes actually sent, if data successfully sent

listitem.para: - 0, if TX is busy. The caller of XUartPsv_Send_NoWait can
wait if return 0.
The number of bytes is not asserted so that this function may be called with
a value of zero to stop an operation that is already in progress.

briefdescription.para: This function attempts to receive a specified number of bytes of data from the device and store it into the specified buffer.

detaileddescription.para: This function works for both polled or interrupt driven modes. It is non-blocking.

In a polled mode, this function will only receive the data already in the RX FIFO. The application may need to call it repeatedly to receive the entire buffer. Polled mode is the default mode of operation for the device.

In interrupt mode, this function will start the receiving, if not the entire buffer has been received, the interrupt handler will continue receiving data until the entire buffer has been received. A callback function, as specified by the application, will be called to indicate the completion of the receiving or error conditions.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance
is pointer to buffer for data to be received into
is the number of bytes to be received. A value of zero will stop a previous receive operation that is in progress in interrupt mode.

simplesect.para: The number of bytes is not asserted so that this function may be called with a value of zero to stop an operation that is already in progress.

briefdescription.para: This function sends a buffer that has been previously specified by setting up the instance variables of the instance.

detaileddescription.para: This function is an internal function for the XUartPsv driver such that it may be called from a shell function that sets up the buffer or from an interrupt handler.
This function sends the specified buffer in either polled or interrupt driven modes. This function is non-blocking.
In a polled mode, this function only sends as much data as the TX FIFO can buffer. The application may need to call it repeatedly to send the entire buffer.

In interrupt mode, this function starts the sending of the buffer, if not the entire buffer has been sent, then the interrupt handler continues the sending until the entire buffer has been sent. A callback function, as specified by the application, will be called to indicate the completion of sending.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance

simplesect.para: The number of bytes actually sent

briefdescription.para: This function receives a buffer that has been previously specified by setting up the instance variables of the instance.

detaileddescription.para: This function is an internal function, and it may be called from a shell function that sets up the buffer or from an interrupt handler.

This function attempts to receive a specified number of bytes from the device and store it into the specified buffer. This function works for either polled or interrupt driven modes. It is non-blocking.

In polled mode, this function only receives as much data as in the RX FIFO. The application may need to call it repeatedly to receive the entire buffer. Polled mode is the default mode for the driver.

In interrupt mode, this function starts the receiving, if not the entire buffer has been received, the interrupt handler will continue until the entire buffer has been received. A callback function, as specified by the application, will be called to indicate the completion of the receiving or error conditions.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance

briefdescription.para: Sets the baud rate for the device.

detaileddescription.para: Checks the input value for validity and also verifies that the requested rate can be configured to within the maximum error range specified by XUARTPSV_MAX_BAUD_ERROR_RATE. If the provided rate is not possible, the current setting is unchanged.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance
to be set

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if everything configured as expected
XST_UART_BAUD_ERROR if the requested rate is not available because there was too much error

briefdescription.para: This function reprograms the control register according to the following sequence mentioned in the TRM.

detaileddescription.para:
Sequence to Program Control Register.
Disable UART
Check if Busy
Flush the transmit FIFO
Program the Control Register
Enable the Uart

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance
value to be written

briefdescription.para: This function is a cleanup function to allow resetting Rx NextBytePtr, RemainingBytes and RequestedBytes.
This function is a cleanup function to allow resetting Tx NextBytePtr, RemainingBytes and RequestedBytes.
Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: The XUartPsv_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration found or NULL if the specified device ID/BaseAddress was not found. See xuartpsv.h for the definition of XUartPsv_Config.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: Sets the options for the specified driver instance.

detaileddescription.para: The options are implemented as bit masks such that multiple options may be enabled or disabled simultaneously.

The GetOptions function may be called to retrieve the currently enabled options. The result is ORed in the desired new settings to be enabled and ANDed with the inverse to clear the settings to be disabled. The resulting value is then used as the options for the SetOption function call.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance.
contains the options to be set which are bit masks contained in the file xuartpsv.h and named XUARTPSV_OPTION_*.

briefdescription.para: Gets the options for the specified driver instance.

detaileddescription.para: The options are implemented as bit masks such that multiple options may be enabled or disabled simultaneously.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance.

simplesect.para: The current options for the UART. The options are bit masks that are contained in the file xuartpsv.h and named XUARTPSV_OPTION_*.

briefdescription.para: This functions sets the Tx and Rx FIFO trigger level to the 'TriggerLevel' argument.

detaileddescription.para: The same value is set for Tx and Rx FIFOs. The receive or transmit trigger level specifies the number of bytes in the FIFO that cause a receive or transmit data event (interrupt) to be generated.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance.
contains the trigger level to set. This is a value from 0-7

briefdescription.para: This functions sets the Tx FIFO trigger level to the 'TriggerLevel' argument.

detaileddescription.para: This value is set for Tx FIFO. Rx FIFO trigger level is unchanged. The receive or transmit trigger level specifies the number of bytes in the FIFO that cause a receive or transmit data event (interrupt) to be generated.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance.
contains the trigger level to set. This is a value from 0-4 (XUARTPSV_UARTIFLS_TXIFLSEL_1_8 - XUARTPSV_UARTIFLS_TXIFLSEL_7_8)

briefdescription.para: This functions sets the Rx FIFO trigger level to the 'TriggerLevel' argument.

detaileddescription.para: This value is set for Rx FIFO. Tx FIFO trigger level is unchanged. The receive or transmit trigger level specifies the number of bytes in the FIFO that cause a receive or transmit data event (interrupt) to be generated.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance.
contains the trigger level to set. This is a value from 0-32 (XUARTPSV_UARTIFLS_RXIFLSEL_1_8 - XUARTPSV_UARTIFLS_RXIFLSEL_7_8)

briefdescription.para: This function gets the Tx and Rx FIFO trigger level.

detaileddescription.para:
The receive or transmit trigger level specifies the number of bytes in the FIFO that cause a receive or transmit data event (interrupt) to be generated.
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance.
The current receive FIFO trigger level. This is a value from 0-63.
None.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the modem status from the specified UART.

detaileddescription.para: The modem status indicates any changes of the modem signals. This function allows the modem status to be read in a polled mode. The modem status is updated whenever it is read such that reading it twice may not yield the same results.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance.

simplesect.para: The modem status which are bit masks that are contained in the file xuartpsv.h and named XUARTPSV_UARTRIS_*.
The bit masks used for the modem status are the exact bits of the modem status register with no abstraction.

briefdescription.para: This function determines if the specified UART is sending data.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance.

listitem.para: TRUE if the UART is sending data
FALSE if UART is not sending data

briefdescription.para: This function gets the operational mode of the UART.

detaileddescription.para: The UART can operate in one of four modes: Normal, Local Loopback, Remote Loopback, or automatic echo.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance.

simplesect.para: The operational mode is specified by constants defined in xuartpsv.h. The constants are named XUARTPSV_OPER_MODE_*

briefdescription.para: This function sets the operational mode of the UART.

detaileddescription.para: The UART can operate in one of four modes: Normal, Local Loopback, Remote Loopback, or automatic echo.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance.
is the mode of the UART.

briefdescription.para: Sets the data format for the device.

detaileddescription.para: The data format includes the baud rate, number of data bits, number of stop bits, and parity. It is the caller's responsibility to ensure that the UART is not sending or receiving data when this function is called.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance.
is a pointer to a format structure containing the data format to be set.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the data format was successfully set.
XST_UART_BAUD_ERROR indicates the baud rate could not be set because of the amount of error with the baud rate and the input clock frequency.
XST_INVALID_PARAM if one of the parameters was not valid.

simplesect.para: The data types in the format type, data bits and parity, are 32 bit fields to prevent a compiler warning. The asserts in this function will cause a warning if these fields are bytes.

briefdescription.para: Gets the data format for the specified UART.

detaileddescription.para: The data format includes the baud rate, number of data bits, number of stop bits, and parity.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance.
is a pointer to a format structure that will contain the data format after this call completes.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the interrupt mask.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance.

simplesect.para: The current interrupt mask. The mask indicates which interrupts are enabled.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the interrupt mask.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance
contains the interrupts to be enabled or disabled. A '1' enables an interrupt, and a '0' disables.

briefdescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the driver.

detaileddescription.para: It must be connected to an interrupt system by the application such that it can be called when an interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: contains a pointer to the driver instance

briefdescription.para: This function sets the handler that will be called when an event (interrupt) occurs that needs application's attention.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance
is the pointer to the callback function.
is the upper layer callback reference passed back when the callback function is invoked.

simplesect.para: There is no assert on the CallBackRef since the driver doesn't know what it is (nor should it)

briefdescription.para: This function runs a self-test on the driver and hardware device.

detaileddescription.para: This self test performs a local loopback and verifies data can be sent and received.
The time for this test is proportional to the baud rate that has been set prior to calling this function.
The mode and control registers are restored before return.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the test was successful
XST_UART_TEST_FAIL if the test failed looping back the data

simplesect.para: This function can hang if the hardware is not functioning properly.

briefdescription.para: This function sends one byte using the device.

detaileddescription.para: This function operates in polled mode and blocks until the data has been put into the TX FIFO register.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device.
contains the byte to be sent.

briefdescription.para: This function receives a byte from the device.

detaileddescription.para: It operates in polled mode and blocks until a byte has received.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device.

detaileddescription.para: (To be implemented if needed)

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device.

briefdescription.para: This function handles the interrupt when there is overrun error in Rx path.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance

briefdescription.para: This function handles the interrupt when data is in RX FIFO.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance

briefdescription.para: This function handles the interrupt when data has been sent, the transmit FIFO is empty (transmitter holding register).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance

detaileddescription.para: It does not do any processing except to call the application handler to indicate a modem event.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance

briefdescription.para: Get the UART Channel Status Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance.

simplesect.para: The value read from the register.

briefdescription.para: Get the UART Mode Control Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance.

simplesect.para: The value read from the register.

briefdescription.para: Set the UART Mode Control Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance.
is the value to be written to the register.

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XUartPsv_SetModeControl(XUartPsv *InstancePtr, u16 RegisterValue)

briefdescription.para: Enable the transmitter and receiver of the UART.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance.

briefdescription.para: Disable the transmitter and receiver of the UART.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance.

briefdescription.para: Determine if the transmitter FIFO is empty.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPsv instance.

listitem.para: TRUE if a byte can be sent
FALSE if the Transmitter Fifo is not empty

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device.
contains the offset from the base address of the device.

simplesect.para: The value read from the register.
C-Style signature: u32 XUartPsv_ReadReg(UINTPTR BaseAddress, int RegOffset)

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device.
contains the offset from the base address of the device.
is the value to be written to the register.

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XUartPsv_WriteReg(UINTPTR BaseAddress, int RegOffset, u16 RegisterValue)

briefdescription.para: Determine if there is receive data in the receiver and/or FIFO.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device.

simplesect.para: TRUE if there is receive data, FALSE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Determine if a byte of data can be sent with the transmitter.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device.

simplesect.para: TRUE if the TX FIFO is full, FALSE if a byte can be put in the FIFO.

briefdescription.para: Determine if a byte of data can be sent with the transmitter.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device.

simplesect.para: TRUE if the TX is busy, FALSE if a byte can be put in the FIFO.

detaileddescription.para: of options
of bytes in transfer

briefdescription.para: The xuartpsv.c file contains the implementation of the interface functions for XUartPsv driver.

detaileddescription.para: The xuartpsv_xfer.h header file contains the prototypes of objects used internally.
The xuartpsv_sinit.c file contains the implementation of the XUartPsv driver's static initialization functionality.
The xuartpsv_selftest.c file contains the self-test functions for the XUartPsv driver.
The xuartpsv_options.c file contains the implementation of the options functions for the XUartPsv driver.
The xuartpsv_intr.c file contains the functions for interrupt handling.
The xuartpsv_hw.h header file contains the hardware interface of an XUartPsv device.
Refer to the header file xuartpsv.h for more detailed information.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sg 09/18/17 First Release 1.2 rna 01/20/20 Add function to Program control register following the sequence mentioned in TRM 1.4 rna 03/12/21 Add read,write of LCR in 'XUartPsv_SetBaudRate' from TRM 03/15/21 Improve the accuracy of FBRD value 1.5 sd 05/04/22 Update the loop XUartPsv_ReceiveBuffer 1.8 sd 10/20/22 Add XUartPsv_CleanupRx and XUartPsv_CleanupTx to reset rx and Tx buffers. 1.14 vlt 01/27/26 Fixed codespell issues

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sg 09/18/17 First Release

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sg 09/18/17 First Release 1.2 rna 01/20/20 Add UARTRSR register bit

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sg 09/18/17 First Release 1.2 rna 01/20/20 Modify the Interrupt sub routine Add support for Rx errors Change the Tx interrupt path to support > 32 byte transfers 1.3 rna 04/05/20 Correct XUartPsv_SetInterruptMask function clear IMSC

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sg 09/18/17 First Releasee 1.2 rna 01/20/20 Use XUartPsv_ProgramCtrlReg function to change mode Add functions to set Tx and Rx FIFO threshold levels 1.3 rna 04/08/20 Format is corrected in XUartPsv_SetDataFormat function rna 05/18/20 Fix MISRA-C violations

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sg 09/18/17 First Release 1.2 rna 01/20/20 Use XUartPsv_ProgramCtrlReg function to restore CR 1.3 rna 05/14/20 Fix Misra-C violations

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sg 09/18/17 First Release 1.14 vlt 12/18/25 Update Doxygen comments to include SDT flow details.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.3 rna 05/18/20 First release
